payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the non-superseded/current memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered because it is superseded, so presenting it as current would violate the rule not to surface superseded facts as active memory. If shown at all, mem_old should only appear as historical/debug context with an explicit superseded label.
payload.decision: Recall should return mem_new as the current durable memory for Sam from Goldman and filter mem_old because mem_old is marked or inferred as superseded by mem_new.

entity_resolution.reason: The query refers to Sam from Goldman, so recall should resolve against memories scoped to the Sam entity associated with Goldman. The provided fixture does not include a concrete entity_id.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory card for Sam from Goldman.
mem_old was not returned as current because it is superseded by mem_new.

details: Preserved superseded status and did not present mem_old as current.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has a superseded_by pointer to mem_new or equivalent inactive status.
Verify that mem_new is marked active/current and scoped to the same Sam from Goldman entity context.
If mem_old and mem_new are not actually the same entity/context, split the entities or remove the supersession link.
If debug output includes mem_old, label it explicitly as superseded rather than current.
